Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Star Alliance opens new Connection Centre at LAX to speed up tight transfers

Star Alliance has opened a new Star Connection Center at Los Angeles International Airport (LAX), its ninth such…...

Dedicated connection facilities reduce operational friction for alliance members, enabling faster turnarounds and improved network efficiency at major hub airports.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

New NTSB footage shows staggering scale of LaGuardia crash tragedy

The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has released footage of the LaGuardia Airport (LGA) crash site from the…...

NTSB's visual documentation establishes investigative baseline data that will inform systemic findings about airport operations, pilot decision-making, or aircraft systems beyond what preliminary reports typically reveal.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

FAA investigates United Airlines 737 and Black Hawk close call in Santa Ana

A California National Guard UH-60M Black Hawk helicopter crossed in front of a United Airlines Boeing 737-800 on…...

Airspace incursions involving military helicopters near major airports suggest potential gaps in VFR corridor awareness or coordination procedures that warrant system-wide review.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 27, 2026

US diverts Swiss F-35 funds to cover frozen Patriot payments

The United States has circumvented a Swiss payment freeze on the Patriot air defense system by redirecting funds…...

U.S. defense procurement faces increasing complexity as allied countries impose financial constraints on military equipment, requiring creative fund-sourcing mechanisms that could signal broader geopolitical shifts in defense partnerships.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to document why chosen controls are appropriate and justify acceptance of remaining risks, moving beyond theoretical analysis to operationalized safety decisions.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Boeing Boost: Major African Carrier Doubles Down On 737 MAX With 10 New Jets

The new aircraft will come quicker than you might expect.

Accelerated delivery schedules signal Boeing's restored production capacity and growing airline confidence in 737 MAX operational reliability post-grounding.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

SAN FRANCISCO – Canadian startup SBQuantum plans to send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit March 30 on a Spire Global satellite flying on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite, ground stations and data processing for SBQuantum’s magnetometer, which is...

Quantum sensing technology entering orbit enables unprecedented precision measurements of Earth's magnetic field with applications for navigation, space weather monitoring, and potential aviation safety enhancements.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

Japanese company ispace is revising its lunar lander design and further delaying the first mission by its American subsidiary while also unveiling plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

ispace's dual strategy of hardware redesign plus lunar communications infrastructure signals a shift toward long-term lunar presence economics beyond single cargo missions.

See SpaceNews →

PAI
Safety Mar 27, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations that psychologically empower frontline workers to report hazards without fear of punishment achieve measurably better safety outcomes than those relying solely on compliance systems.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Qantas Scraps Nonstop Perth-Paris Route In Major European Network Shake-Up

Qantas shakes up its international network to offer more flights to Europe via Singapore.

Qantas is pivoting from ultra-long-haul direct routes to a hub-and-spoke model through Singapore, trading frequency and connectivity for operational efficiency.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Lockheed Martin F-35 workers open contract talks in Fort Worth

The union representing around 5,000 workers building the F-35 at Lockheed Martin’s Fort Worth, Texas, plant has opened…...

Labor negotiations at a major F-35 production facility could impact defense contractor supply chain stability and aircraft delivery timelines across multiple military programs.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The MD-11 & DC-10

Though closely related, the MD-11 and DC-10 differed in range, cockpit design, aerodynamics, and ultimately commercial success.

The MD-11's modern avionics and aerodynamic refinements enabled significantly extended range over its DC-10 predecessor, yet struggled commercially due to late 1980s market timing and higher operating costs.

See Simple Flying →

▶
Safety Mar 27, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading helicopter killer because pilots systematically overestimate their ability to detect and react to unmarked wires at operational altitudes and speeds.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 27, 2026

TEKEVER successfully integrates Quadsat Electronic Warfare Payload SpectraLoc onto AR3 EVO Drone Platform

London, England — TEKEVER, a leading provider of advanced AI-based autonomous systems, and Quadsat have successfully...

Integration of electronic warfare payloads onto tactical drones expands ISR capabilities beyond traditional surveillance, enabling real-time signal detection and threat identification for military operations.

See sUAS News →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The US Domestic Routes Operated By Widebody Aircraft

Some of the country's busiest domestic routes are operated by widebodies.

Widebody deployment on domestic routes reflects airlines' capacity management strategy during peak demand periods, maximizing revenue on high-traffic corridors.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

United Airlines Jet & Military Helicopter Separated By Only 500 Feet Over Orange County

A close call in the sunny skies of SoCal.

The incident highlights systemic vulnerabilities in airspace separation protocols when military and civilian traffic operate in proximity without adequate coordination infrastructure.

See Simple Flying →

▶
General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from training aircraft to air carriers demonstrates the operational complexity required of medium-sized regional hubs.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Satellite Spots a Spawn

The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

Remote sensing satellites demonstrate increasing sensitivity to detect biological phenomena, creating new opportunities for environmental monitoring applications beyond traditional aerospace observation missions.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER

The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.

While the A350-1000's marginal range advantage appears significant on paper, operational factors like payload-range tradeoffs and fuel efficiency in real-world conditions may neutralize this benefit for airlines.

See Simple Flying →

eVTOL Mar 27, 2026

AIR claims $1 billion in orders as eVTOL maker pushes personal aircraft

Israel-based electric aircraft developer AIR has declared that it has surpassed $1 billion in orders, driven by demand…...

AIR's $1 billion order book demonstrates eVTOL manufacturers are translating early enthusiasm into substantial pre-orders, signaling institutional confidence in near-term commercialization timelines.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight systems offers immediate capacity gains and emissions reductions without waiting for next-generation aircraft technology.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The GE9X Is Compared To The GE90

The engine is set to enter commercial service on the upcoming Boeing 777X.

The GE9X's significant size increase over its predecessor enables the 777X to achieve better fuel efficiency and range despite higher thrust demands from larger aircraft designs.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

U.S. military surveillance coordination in GEO orbit demonstrates advanced satellite maneuvering capabilities essential for monitoring strategic space assets amid growing orbital congestion.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

8 Hours In A Boeing 737 MAX? Inside United Airlines' New Longest Nonstop Flights

Half of the ten longest routes started in 2025 or will do so in 2026. See them all here!

United's aggressive expansion of ultra-long 737 MAX routes signals carrier confidence in the aircraft's reliability and passenger appetite for point-to-point connectivity over traditional hub-and-spoke models.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

I'm A Flight Attendant, And These Are The Things I Wish Every Passenger Knew Before Boarding

Practical advice for passengers that makes flights easier.

Flight attendants prioritize safety procedures and passenger cooperation far more than comfort conveniences, yet many travelers remain unaware of how their behavior directly impacts crew efficiency and emergency response capabilities.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

New Route & 9 More Boosted: JetBlue Keeps Turning Up The Heat In Fort Lauderdale

JetBlue adds Cleveland and boosts nine routes as Fort Lauderdale growth accelerates again.

JetBlue's focused hub expansion at Fort Lauderdale demonstrates how point-to-point carriers can compete with legacy airlines through strategic capacity additions rather than traditional hub-and-spoke models.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Space Command begins phased move to Alabama

The new headquarters building is expected to be completed by 2031...

Space Command's relocation to Alabama represents a significant shift in U.S. military space operations infrastructure, with aerospace contractors competing for contracts to support the new hub through 2031 and beyond.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April 2026 offers optimal viewing conditions for multiple celestial events that can enhance pilot situational awareness and passenger engagement during night operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has completed construction and is entering final pre-launch testing phases, positioning the observatory for deployment within the next 1-2 years.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space

While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.

Government space procurement shifts toward commercial models, but purchasing volume constraints limit actual revenue growth for commercial suppliers despite increased contract flexibility.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion and testing phase positions it for near-term launch, marking a major step toward NASA's next flagship observatory after JWST.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

Artemis II's international crew composition signals NASA's strategic pivot toward sustained lunar exploration partnerships beyond traditional U.S.-only missions.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding, as it looks to expand its…...

AI-powered pilot training platforms are attracting significant early-stage investment, signaling industry recognition that simulation-based instruction could reduce training costs and timelines for commercial operators.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

ISS thermal management requires specialized expertise to maintain experiment integrity during cargo transport, creating niche career opportunities in space logistics.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows

While the space economy is expanding across multiple fronts, questions remain about how some of its most promising opportunities could translate growing demand into sustainable profits.

Space industry leaders must develop viable monetization strategies beyond capacity expansion to achieve long-term financial sustainability amid surging demand.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Airbus Tests Multi-Aircraft Aerial Firefighting System

Trial linked aircraft, drones and ground crews for enhanced targeting and coordination.

Networked aerial firefighting systems could reduce response times and improve resource efficiency by enabling real-time coordination between crewed and uncrewed aircraft.
